title: Telegram sends literal '(empty)' to user when LLM returns blank response
source: Ash live report 2026-06-22 â€” Pi sent '(empty)' as a message to Ash mid-conversation. Looks broken and confusing.
current_state: _send_plain_chunks (used for media responses: photo, video, voice, document) calls _chunk_text(text or '(empty)', 4096) â€” if process_input returns '' or None, the literal string '(empty)' is sent to Telegram as Pi's reply. Similarly, _reply_chunks (text response path) would send '' if process_input returns empty, which shows as an empty bubble in Telegram. The '(empty)' fallback was added as a safety net to prevent empty send_message calls, but it is user-visible and looks like a bug to Ash.
target_state: When Pi's response is blank: (1) for _send_plain_chunks (media path), send 'Something went wrong â€” please try again.' instead of '(empty)'; (2) for _reply_chunks (text path), if the formatted chunk is empty after formatting, skip sending it (don't send an empty bubble). Additionally, if process_input itself returns '' from a provider error, the error message from the RuntimeError handler in _respond_via_config is already non-empty â€” trace the actual cause of blank responses and add a guard at the source rather than just at the send site.
migration_plan: 1. _send_plain_chunks: change `text or '(empty)'` to `text or 'Something went wrong â€” please try again.'`.
2. _reply_chunks: after `formatted = _format_for_telegram(text)`, if not formatted.strip(), return None (skip send). Log a warning via track_silent.
3. In _dispatch_bubble: if reply (process_input result) is empty/blank, send a fallback message instead of silently sending empty. Fallback: 'I hit an error â€” send that again?'

4. Tests: test_telegram_empty_response.py â€” (a) _send_plain_chunks with '' sends the fallback message, not '(empty)'; (b) _reply_chunks with '' returns None and does not call bot.send_message; (c) _dispatch_bubble with empty process_input result sends the fallback.
risk_notes: The '(empty)' string is referenced in test_telegram_native_actions.py (test_reply_chunks_returns_last_message_id) â€” update that test to use non-empty text so the test still passes after this change.
